(SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)***
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED (TRANSACTION ID # 62861828) FILED BY DEFENDANT LIPSON, MICHAEL

LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIPSON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CCP SEC. 430.41 REQUIRES A DECLARATION REPORTING THAT THE PARTIES HAVE MET AND CONFERRED BEFORE A DEMURRER MAY BE FILED. SUCH A DECLARATION WAS NOT FILED IN THIS INSTANCE, SO THE DEMURRER IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND DEFENDANT HAS UNTIL SEPTEMBER 9, 2019 TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ADRIANA ROBLES, CSR #14212, E-MAIL: AROBLES@SFTC.ORG (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIPSON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. (SEE CODE CIV. PROC., 430.10(E).) PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLEARLY PLEAD THE TERMS OF THE CONTRACT. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEC-06-2024 HEARING ON MOTION TO DISMISS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 583.360 FOR FAILURE TO BRING CASE TO TRIAL. THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO LAW AND MOTION ON JAN-08-2025 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 301. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER REGARDING THE CONTINUANCE AND SUBMIT IT VIA EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2019 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIPSON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant Michael Lipson's demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained with leave to amend. (See Code Civ. Proc., 430.10(e).) Plaintiff has leave to amend to clearly plead the terms of the contract. The amended complaint presently refers to a 1990 written agreement but attaches an agreement that states that it will become effective in 2000. Plaintiff has leave to clarify the terms of the contract, e.g., whether the attached document superseded the parties' original agreement. The Court rejects Defendant's argument that Plaintiff must plead the terms of its merger agreement with predecessor-in-interest FIA Card Services, N.A. in order to establish standing to sue; the allegations of the complaint on that issue are sufficient.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIPSON's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is adopted by the Court as follows:

Despite this court's prior order, counsel apparently have still failed to comply with CCP 430.41. That statute requires a meet and confer "in person or by telephone" to determine "whether an agreement can be reached that would resolve the objections to be raised in the demurrer." Plaintiff's counsel declares that a phone call occurred, but defendant's counsel "did not specify what causes of action to which he intended to demur, nor did he provide the basis on which he intended to demur." Defendant's counsel's declaration is not to the contrary. Counsel are once again admonished to obey CCP 430.41 and the DEMURRER IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately. Defendant has until November 18, 2019 to respond to the first amended complaint.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIPSON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: CCP Sec. 430.41 requires a declaration reporting that the parties have met and conferred before a demurrer may be filed. Such a declaration was not filed in this instance, so the demurrer is ordered OFF CALENDAR. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant has until September 9, 2019 to respond to the complaint.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Adriana Robles, CSR #14212, e-mail: arobles@sftc.org (302/RBU)
